markdown:
<definition term="Economic Integration">**Cooperation and coordination **between countries and their economic policies, to make them more economically **interconnected.**</definition>

Countries hope to **derive benefits** through cooperating as they could:

1. Start by** lowering trade restrictions** between nations and minor economic barriers.
2. This leads to very strong integration of **policies and markets.**

There are different degrees of these **economic** **integrations **which determine how** interconnected **certain economies are to each other. This is discussed below.

# Preferential Trade Agreements


<definition term="Preferential Trade Agreements">An agreement between countries to **reduce** the amount of **trade restrictions** imposed between them on **specific products**.</definition>

A **PTA,** oftentimes are settled on **certain products**, whilst **other products** can still have trade barriers.

1. PTAs make it **easier** for member (part of PTA) countries to **access the foreign market** of the specific **goods** compared to non-member nations.
2. It could involve **cooperation** between **nations** in **specific economic policies **and take **multiple forms** as discussed below.

The goal for **all these agreements** is ultimately to achieve **trade liberalisation**.

<definition term="Trade Liberalization">To **free up** international trade by **removing** trade barriers.</definition>

### Bilateral Trade Agreements


1. **Bilateral trade agreements** involve **two countries** agreeing to **reduce** or **eliminate** trade barriers.
2. These agreements are **customised** to meet the specific **needs** and **interests** of the **two** parties involved.

<callout type="example">The **Closer Economic Partnership Arrangement (CEPA) **between China and Hong Kong, signed in 2004, aimed to **enhance** cross-border trade and investment by **reducing** **tariffs** and **non**-**tariff** barriers.</callout>

### Regional Trade Agreements


1. **Regional trade agreements (RTAs)** involve **multiple countries** within a **specific** geographic area.
2. These agreements aim to **promote** economic integration (**foster closer economic ties**)** **by **reducing** trade **barriers** among member states and **taking advantage** of their closeness in distance.

### Multilateral Trade Agreements


1. **Multilateral trade agreements** involve **three or more countries** and are often **facilitated** by international organisations.
2. These agreements **aim **to create a **global** framework for trade by **reducing** **barriers** on a **larger** scale.

<callout type="note">Multilateral agreements often take **years** to **negotiate** due to the **diverse** **interests** of **participating** countries. This complexity can **delay** the **realisation** of benefits.</callout>

<callout type="warning">Students often confuse **regional **and **multilateral **agreements! Remember, regional agreements focus on **specific** **geographic** areas, while multilateral agreements involve **countries** from **different** regions.</callout>

#### The Role of the World Trade Organisation (WTO)


The **WTO** includes many countries leading to all the agreements being **multilateral** and aiming for trade liberalisation.

1. It encourages the reduction of trade barriers between countries with a **non-discriminating** objective.
2. Therefore, countries cannot impose **different **restrictions on **different** nations _(with the exception of some regional and bilateral agreements)_.
3. It provides a **platform** for **resolving** **trade** conflicts between **member** **states** and offers **technical assistance** and flexibility in **implementing **trade rules.

The **WTO** is discussed in further detail in _4.4.6 The World Trade Organisation_.

<callout type="tok">How can **political relations** between countries **determine** which people in the world **deserve** to **gain** or **lose**?</callout>

<callout type="self_review">Can you identify a real-world **example** of each **type** of trade **agreement**? What are the **key** **benefits** and **drawbacks** of each?</callout>
